t_ch: 出来！是谁，快出来啊！
t_en: Who's there? Show yourself!

t_ch: 可惜，对手实在太警觉了。追踪失败，再来一次吧。
t_en: Alas, the opponent is too vigilant. The tracking fails. Time for another try.

t_ch: 那家伙发现我们了吗？快躲起来。
t_en: Did he spot us? Quickly, hide!

t_ch: <size=36>别疑神疑鬼了！时间紧迫，快过来。</size>
t_en: <size=36>Don't be so paranoid! We're running out of time. Get over here!</size>

t_ch: Xuu harr，我来了！
t_en: Xuu harr, I'm coming!

t_ch: 他们要离开了，快些跟上他们！
t_en: They're leaving. We should catch up to them, quickly!

t_ch: 小心，他们警觉起来了！
t_en: Watch out! They're on high alert!

t_ch: 这些家伙怎么又折回来了？快退后！
t_en: Why are they coming back? Quickly, retreat!

t_ch: 我就觉得古怪，空气里总有贱畜的味道…
t_en: I knew something was off. There's always this stench of lowly beasts in the air...

t_ch: 果然，这三个小东西一直咬着我们不放。
t_en: Looks like those brats were following us the whole time.

t_ch: 我就觉得古怪，空气里总有贱畜的味道…
t_en: I knew something was off. There's always this stench of lowly beasts in the air...

t_ch: 果然，这三个小东西一直咬着我们不放。
t_en: Looks like those brats were following us the whole time.

t_ch: 这些家伙怎么又折回来了？快退后！
t_en: Why are they coming back? Quickly, retreat!

t_ch: 他们到底要干什么？这些人身着的全都是官方制服，但我敢打赌，这些家伙绝对不是天舶司、工造司或是云骑军的人…太可疑了。
t_en: What are they up to? They're all wearing official uniforms, but I'm pretty sure they're not members of the Sky-Faring Commission, the Artisanship Commission, or the Cloud Knights... This is way too suspicious.

t_ch: 不管他们是谁，把他们的行动都拍下来作为证据。要是有什么罪证，那可就抵赖不了了。
t_en: Never mind who they are, let's just film them. That way, if they do anything bad, we'll have solid evidence against them.

t_ch: 不管他们是谁，把他们的行动都拍下来作为证据。
t_en: Never mind who they are, let's just film them.

t_ch: 拍我干什么？拍他们啊？
t_en: Why are you filming me? Shouldn't you be filming them?

t_ch: 瞧瞧，一艘货运星槎。至少够送走二十名弟兄。我还会联络其他成员，让他们准备更多。
t_en: Look at this, a freight starskiff with enough room to fit at least twenty of my men. I'll let the others know and have them prepare more starskiffs.

t_ch: 等脱离关口后，会有接应的兽舰。
t_en: Once we're past the checkpoint, there will be beast ships waiting for us.

t_ch: 末度大人已经准备妥当，我们的古老血脉能否重振，将在此一举……
t_en: Lord Mok Tok is ready. The revival of our ancient bloodline all hinges on this operation...

t_ch: 他说什么？…兽舰？
t_en: What did he just say?... Beast ships?
Who's there!?

t_ch: 是那三个小崽子！我说过了，让你及时善后，你就是不听，白痴！
t_en: It's those brats! I told you to get rid of them, but you didn't listen, you idiot!

t_ch: 不能留活口，动手！
t_en: Wipe them all out!

t_ch: 步离人？！
t_en: Borisin!?

t_ch: Adugusu！受死吧，贱畜！
t_en: Adusa! Die, you lowly beasts!

t_ch: 怎么可能！这些狐人…这些狐人为什么会变了模样？
t_en: How is this possible? How did these foxians... change their appearances like that!?

t_ch: 这些家伙根本不是狐人。他们只是露出了原形。这些家伙是货真价实的步离人，和我在公司那艘舰船上击败的劫匪是同一群害虫。
t_en: They're not foxians at all. They revealed their true form. They're borisin, just like the bandits I defeated on the IPC ship.

t_ch: 那岂不是意味着……
t_en: Wait, that means...

t_ch: 有步离人潜入仙舟…
t_en: The borisin have infiltrated the Xianzhou...

t_ch: 这些步离人是怎么渗透进仙舟的？
t_en: How did the borisin manage to infiltrate the Xianzhou?

t_ch: 步离人怎么变成狐人的模样？
t_en: How did the borisin disguise themselves as foxians?

t_ch: 这些家伙到底施了什么障眼法？咱们刚才追踪他们时，他们看起来和一般的仙舟狐人毫无区别……
t_en: What kind of trick did they use? When we were tracking them earlier, they looked just like normal Xianzhou foxians...

t_ch: 这不是简单换上仙舟的服装，剃去须发的伪装。这几个步离人不知用了什么手法，将自己的模样变得与狐人毫无二致。
t_en: It's not just a simple disguise of wearing our clothing and shaving their whiskers. They're somehow able to alter their appearance to be indistinguishable from foxians.

t_ch: 他们还有着官方身份。天舶司、工造司、还有…云骑军？
t_en: They even have official IDs for the Sky-Faring Commission, the Artisanship Commission, and... even the Cloud Knights?

t_ch: 让我看看这个冒牌云骑的腰牌，或许能有些眉目。
t_en: Let me check this fake Cloud Knight's tag. Maybe it'll give us some clues.

t_ch: 路君？巡防卫队的值守武官？……等等！
t_en: Lujun? An officer of the patrol defense squad... Wait!

t_ch: 怎么了？
t_en: What's the matter?

t_ch: 我曾经见过另一个名叫路君的值守武官，那是在几周之前，押送步离人犯的时候。
t_en: I encountered a patrol officer named Lujun before. It was a few weeks ago when we were transporting the borisin prisoners.

t_ch: 拥有伪造的官方身份，能堂而皇之地出入仙舟…糟糕，真是太糟糕了。
t_en: If they can forge official identities and move around the Xianzhou without raising suspicion... Oh no, this is bad.

t_ch: 更糟糕的是，如果我在列车上发现了一只蟑螂，那就意味着……
t_en: Even worse. If you find one cockroach on the Express, it usually means...

t_ch: 还有更多步离人潜伏在仙舟内。他们的阴谋恐怕不是窃取情报这般小打小闹的事情。
t_en: There are more borisin hiding on the Xianzhou. I bet their plan is much bigger than just stealing information.

t_ch: 必须尽快将这件事报告给神策府！
t_en: We've got to report this to the Seat of Divine Foresight!

t_ch: 与此同时，在神策府中……
t_en: Meanwhile, in the Seat of Divine Foresight...

t_ch: 走吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 可是那几个小崽子…
t_en: But those little brats...

t_ch: 百闻不如一见，星穹列车的客人。我是曜青仙舟的将军，飞霄。
t_en: I am glad to finally meet you in person, guests from the Astral Express. I'm Feixiao, the general of the Xianzhou Yaoqing.

t_ch: 我来为天击将军介绍一番。身着青衣的这位——
t_en: Let me introduce our guests to you. The one dressed in green...

t_ch: 走前边这位是饮月君的隔世之身，跟在他后面的是列车组最近吸纳的新成员，二位鼎鼎大名，我这对耳朵听得可清楚了。
t_en: He's the reincarnation of Imbibitor Lunae, and the person behind him is the newest member of the Crew. I've heard a lot about you.

t_ch: 除开罗浮报告中的记述，「曜青」天舶司也搜罗了不少关于二位的见闻。本人一直想会会你们，原因…想必景元将军也解释过了吧？
t_en: Outside the reports from the Luofu, the Sky-Faring Commission of the Yaoqing has also gathered plenty of information about both of you. I've been eager to meet you face to face, for reasons that... I'm sure General Jing Yuan has explained, right?

t_ch: 我们作为证人前来回答问题。
t_en: Yeah, we're here to answer your questions as witnesses.

t_ch: 不错，但不是堂前会审。只是想请两位聊聊，廓清些事实。
t_en: That's right, but don't worry, this isn't a trial. I just want to have a chat with you and get a better understanding of the facts.

t_ch: 将军没告诉我们你想做什么…
t_en: General Jing Yuan didn't tell us about your intentions at all...

t_ch: 喔？这倒不像我认识的神策将军啊。
t_en: He didn't? That doesn't sound like the Divine Foresight I know.

t_ch: 两位尽管据实回答，无需担心。
t_en: Don't worry. Just tell her the truth.

t_ch: 不就是为了建木重生的事？
t_en: You're here about the Ambrosial Arbor's resurrection, no?

t_ch: 正是。直来直往，我喜欢。
t_en: Straight to the point. I like it.

t_ch: 景元将军在呈递的报告中，将建木灾异的祸首归罪于{RUBY_B#反物质军团}烬灭军团{RUBY_E#}，警示众天将应当关注烬灭祸祖的动向。
t_en: According to General Jing Yuan's report, the {RUBY_B#Antimatter Legion}Ruin Legion{RUBY_E#} is to blame for the Ambrosial Arbor crisis, and all Arbiter-Generals should pay attention to the Ruin Author's movements.

t_ch: 这些年来，「毁灭」的爪牙摧残了数之不尽的世界，联盟也有所防备，只是未曾想他们竟会与「丰饶」的残党联手。
t_en: Over the years, the Destruction's minions have wreaked havoc on countless worlds, and the Alliance has been keeping an eye on them. But no one expected them to join hands with the remnants of the Abundance.

t_ch: 这场建木灾异造成的损害远比预想中低，可以说是不幸中的万幸。但反过来看…也和烬灭军团所到之处荼毒生灵的风格大相径庭。
t_en: The damage caused by the Ambrosial Arbor crisis was far less severe than expected, which is good news for us. However... it was quite different from the Ruin Legion's usual style of destroying life wherever they go.

t_ch: 我自然相信神策将军与各位无名客的英勇事迹，但也不免对报告未能详述的一些细节感到好奇。借此机会，希望能与二位交流一番。
t_en: While I trust the bravery of the Divine Foresight and the Nameless, I'm curious about some details missing from the report. I'd like to take this chance to have an exchange with both of you.

t_ch: 有言在先，我所问的未必是我认为的。提问若有冒犯，还请不要见怪。
t_en: Let me be clear, the questions I'll ask might not reflect my actual thoughts. So, please don't take offense if any of my questions seem a bit harsh.

t_ch: 将军但问无妨。只是我们能回答的，也只是我们所知的；而您所问的…或许内心也早有答案。
t_en: Please go ahead, General. But keep in mind we can only answer based on what we know. And perhaps... you already have the answers to your questions in your heart.

t_ch: 伶牙俐齿，我喜欢。
t_en: You have a clever tongue. I like it.

t_ch: （这天击将军说起话来竟也头头是道。眼下她来意不明，景元又希望我们据实回答…<color=#dbc291ff>也许只说知道的事实就好？</color>）
t_en: (The Merlin's Claw is quite articulate. Right now, her intentions are unknown, and Jing Yuan wants us to be honest... <color=#dbc291ff>Maybe I'll just stick to the facts we know?</color>)

t_ch: 我就开门见山了：起初，星穹列车受通缉重犯星核猎手的引导前来此地，试图解决星核危机。
t_en: Let's cut to the chase. Before the crisis struck, the Astral Express was guided here by a Stellaron Hunter, a wanted felon, in an attempt to resolve the Stellaron Crisis.

t_ch: 可凡在银河行走的，是个人都应听说过星核猎手的昭彰恶名。各位无名客为何如此相信他们的一面之词，莫非是有人沾亲带故？
t_en: However, everyone in the Cosmos knows of the Stellaron Hunters' reputation. So, why did you place so much trust in them? Could it be that some of you have a connection with them?

t_ch: 一切与星核有关
t_en: It all comes down to the Stellaron.

t_ch: 除罗浮外，宇宙中还有不少遭受星核侵蚀的世界。列车之前停靠的雅利洛-VI就是其中之一。
t_en: Apart from the Luofu, there are many other worlds suffering from Stellaron corrosion. For example, Jarilo-VI, the world that the Express stopped at before reaching the Luofu, was one of them.

t_ch: 对于列车而言，星核的存在会阻塞银轨，加剧跃迁风险。因此处理星核引起的麻烦，也是无名客的分内之事。
t_en: To the Express, Stellarons act as roadblocks on the silver rail and pose risks to the warping process. And that's why dealing with Stellaron issues is part of the duty of the Nameless.

t_ch: 唔，星核造成的那些问题我也有所耳闻，列车联结诸界，理应对此做出应对。银河破破烂烂，「开拓」缝缝补补。言之成理。
t_en: Ah, I've heard about those problems caused by Stellarons. The Express connects various worlds, so it makes sense for you to take care of this. The Cosmos is a mess, and the Trailblazers are just doing their best to fix it. I understand.

t_ch: 列车就是大善人
t_en: We are the good guys.

t_ch: 难道列车就不能做些善事？我们这一路上帮助过的世界都快数…倒也不至于数不清，但总之，有很多很多！
t_en: What's wrong with doing something good? Along the way, we've helped countless worlds... well, not literally countless, but you get the idea!

t_ch: 星核猎手警告我们，如果不前往仙舟，至少会有一半罗浮住民死于非命。
t_en: The Stellaron Hunters warned us that if we didn't hurry to the Xianzhou Luofu, half of its inhabitants would die.

t_ch: 有心行善，值得赞赏。云骑巡猎讨魔，也秉持向善之心。虽然总有人不明事理，多加非议，但我不会质疑各位无名客的动机。
t_en: Your good intentions are commendable, just like the Cloud Knights' determination to eliminate those abominations. Even though there are always people who remain skeptical, I won't question your motives.

t_ch: 猜测你和星核猎手的关系
t_en: Raise her suspicion about your relationship with Stellaron Hunters.

t_ch: 你说的不错，也许我确实和星核猎手有所关联……
t_en: You know what? Maybe I do have a connection with Stellaron Hunters...

t_ch: 哦…这倒是有趣。
t_en: Oh...? Now that's interesting.

t_ch: 看来你和星核猎手关系匪浅。但这群通缉犯形迹可疑，你这样诚实自白，我倒是欣赏，只是你得明白，这可是在往自己身上揽嫌疑喔？
t_en: Looks like you're quite close to the Stellaron Hunters. However, I must warn you that they are wanted felons. While I appreciate your honesty, being honest about this topic might make you look suspicious to some, you know?

t_ch: 谈谈艾利欧的预言
t_en: Talk about Elio's prophecy.

t_ch: 据传，星核猎手的首领艾利欧拥有目视未来众多可能性的力量。
t_en: Rumor has it that Elio, the leader of the Stellaron Hunters, possesses the power to see into the future.

t_ch: 他预见仙舟和列车都将参与对抗纳努克的战争。而将我们引来解决罗浮的星核危机，正是为了实现这一预言。
t_en: He foresaw that the Xianzhou and the Express would have important roles to play in the war against Nanook. That's why we were lured here to the Luofu, to deal with the Stellaron crisis and fulfill the prophecy.

t_ch: 从报告来看，景元将军也相信了这一说辞。这倒令我好奇，为何你们对此并无质疑？
t_en: General Jing Yuan believes in this prophecy too, as mentioned in the report. I'm curious why you didn't question it at all?

t_ch: 难道是因为猎手中还有一位将军的{RUBY_B#刃}旧相识{RUBY_E#}？
t_en: Could it be because one of the Stellaron Hunters is actually {RUBY_B#Blade}an old acquaintance{RUBY_E#} of General Jing Yuan?

t_ch: 天击将军请慎言。对无名客的问话不应变成同僚间的猜疑。那名星核猎手也曾是老朽的弟子，你是否也要考验我的忠诚？
t_en: Please be cautious with your words, Merlin's Claw. Let's avoid sowing doubt among our comrades. That Stellaron Hunter used to be my disciple, so are you putting my loyalty in question, too?

t_ch: 炎老，我说的这些正是联盟内部对景元将军的非议。只是今日他们借我之口问了出来，要不您把我的样子想像成那几个老家伙的嘴脸？
t_en: I'm simply bringing up the doubts about General Jing Yuan that exist within the Alliance. Since I'm representing them, perhaps you can just imagine me as one of those old geezers?

t_ch: 接着，报告提及「药王秘传」魁首丹枢心怀不轨，与绝灭大君勾结，动用星核复苏了建木。
t_en: Let's move on to the next question. The report suggests that Dan Shu, the Master of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, colluded with the Lord Ravager and used the power of the Stellaron to resurrect the Ambrosial Arbor.

t_ch: 可丹枢不过一介丹士长，竟如此手眼通天。她勾结外敌，召来星核也就罢了，古海建木可是持明一族的领地，她是如何绕过守卫的？
t_en: But here's the thing: Dan Shu was just a chief alchemist. Even if she colluded with our enemies and summoned the Stellaron, how did she manage to bypass the Vidyadhara guards around the Ambrosial Arbor?

t_ch: 罗浮龙师颇为可疑
t_en: The Preceptors of the Luofu seem suspicious.

t_ch: 还能为什么？显然是罗浮龙师监守自盗，抛弃了守望建木的责任，与药王秘传勾结。
t_en: The answer seems rather simple, doesn't it? The Preceptors of the Luofu abandoned their duty to guard the Ambrosial Arbor and colluded with the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus.

t_ch: 这倒也不是没可能。但为何不是景元将军将流放许久的丹恒先生带回了罗浮，丹恒分开古海，才致使建木失控重生。
t_en: That's a possibility, but there's another scenario to consider: General Jing Yuan brought back Mr. Dan Heng, who had been in exile for a long time. As a result, Dan Heng parted the Seas, eventually leading to the resurrection of the Ambrosial Arbor.

t_ch: 我原本没有擅入仙舟的打算，只是……
t_en: I never intended to trespass on the Xianzhou ship, but...

t_ch: 你既然没有擅入仙舟的打算，那为何在流放令终止前却重返仙舟？
t_en: If you had no intention to trespass, then why did you return to the Xianzhou ship before your exile was lifted?

t_ch: 是我担心伙伴的安危，不得不擅自行动。相关罪责，应当由丹恒一人承担。
t_en: I was concerned about the safety of my companions, so I had to act brazenly. I alone bear the blame.

t_ch: 丹恒先生不必紧张，若是真有罪责，这也不是你一人能担起的。
t_en: Please don't worry, Mr. Dan Heng. If someone must be held accountable, it won't fall solely on your shoulders.

t_ch: 我曾见过丹枢本人
t_en: I've personally met Dan Shu.

t_ch: 我和丹枢有过一面之缘。她的挚友死于方壶的战争，她憎恨「巡猎」…在丹鼎司中准备多年，正是为了向仙舟复仇。
t_en: I personally met Dan Shu once. Her closest friend was killed during the war on the Fanghu, and she harbored deep hatred towards The Hunt. So she spent years making preparations in the Alchemy Commission, in order to take revenge on the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 复仇…这同样是一种「巡猎」。但这不意味着她就有能力将星核带入持明把守的鳞渊境。
t_en: Revenge... is also a form of The Hunt. However, that doesn't explain how she managed to bring the Stellaron into the Scalegorge Waterscape, which was guarded by the Vidyadhara.

t_ch: 这个问题应该由丹枢自己回答。
t_en: Well, you should ask Dan Shu herself for the answer.

t_ch: 可惜丹枢已死，就连尸骸也碎成了一捧飞灰。可追究的线索又少了一个。
t_en: Unfortunately, Dan Shu is dead, and even her corpse has crumbled into ash. That's one less clue we can pursue.

t_ch: 放逐饮月君，才有今日祸患
t_en: The exile of Imbibitor Lunae is what led to this calamity.

t_ch: 要不是罗浮放逐了丹恒，持明族群龙无首，化作一盘散沙，星核又怎会被神不知鬼不觉地放入建木玄根中？
t_en: If the Luofu hadn't exiled Dan Heng, leaving the Vidyadhara with no leader, they wouldn't have fallen into chaos, and there wouldn't have been the opportunity to bring the Stellaron to the Roots of the Arbor unnoticed.

t_ch: 饮月君被放逐，是他自取其咎。丹恒作为其转世之身，往罪早已一笔勾销。他为何要在流放令尚未解除时就冒险登上罗浮？
t_en: Imbibitor Lunae was exiled due to his own crimes. Now, Dan Heng, as his reincarnation, is free from his sins. However, why did he risk trespassing on the Xianzhou Luofu before his exile was lifted?

t_ch: 我担心伙伴安危，擅自行动，确有不是。但……
t_en: I was concerned about the safety of my companions, so I acted brazenly. I admit I was reckless at that time, but...

t_ch: 但你还是回来了。从结果来看，建木重生，罗浮需要一位龙尊再度编织封印，压抑祸迹。其中利害，也难免令人浮想联翩啊。
t_en: But you returned despite everything. Now let's consider the outcome: The Arbor was resurrected, and the Luofu required a high elder to repair the seal and suppress the plaguemark. It's hard not to think about the stakes involved, right?

t_ch: 拒绝回答
t_en: Refuse to answer anything.

t_ch: 这个问题不该由我们来回答。我们只是来解决星核危机的，丹枢的阴谋细节，无名客怎么会知道啊？
t_en: I don't believe we are in a position to answer this question. We Nameless came here to resolve the Stellaron crisis, and we cannot be expected to know the details of Dan Shu's conspiracy.

t_ch: 说的不错，这才是常人的反应。这个问题就放过二位吧。
t_en: You make a valid point. That's how innocent people should react. Let's skip this question for now.

t_ch: 报告宣称，绝灭大君幻胧是阴谋的策动者。
t_en: According to the report, Lord Ravager Phantylia is the mastermind behind the entire conspiracy.

t_ch: 在与你们一行同路时，她以天舶司某位接渡使的身份示人，而后又蒸发得无影无踪。这祸首倒是来去自由，拿来戴罪背锅也忒方便了。
t_en: She disguised herself as an amicassador of the Sky-Faring Commission and traveled with you, only to vanish without a trace later on. It seems too convenient to label her as a scapegoat.

t_ch: 太卜符玄目击了全过程
t_en: Fu Xuan, the Master Diviner, witnessed everything firsthand.

t_ch: 我们与罗浮的符太卜一起行动。她也见证了幻胧露出真面目。你该不会连自己人也不信吧？
t_en: Back then, we were working with Diviner Fu, and she also witnessed Phantylia's true form. You're not going to doubt one of your own people, are you?

t_ch: 太卜符玄是罗浮重臣，她的证词自然可以信任。但…即便是本人亲眼所见依然有被蒙蔽的可能。祸首来去自由，又离奇蒸发，当然让人心生疑惑。
t_en: Of course not, we can trust the testimony of Master Diviner Fu Xuan. However... even if she saw it with her own eyes, we can't completely rule out the possibility that she might have been deceived. After all, it's suspicious how the culprit just bizarrely vanished.

t_ch: 幻胧是岁阳一族
t_en: Phantylia is a heliobus.

t_ch: 幻胧是曾和仙舟为敌的能量生命，「岁阳」的一员。这东西变化无常，难以捉摸。
t_en: Phantylia is one of the heliobi, the energy lifeforms that once fought against the Xianzhou. They're known for their unpredictable and elusive nature.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}小朋友说的没错。这次建木重生，根须蔓延，还凿穿了罗浮的造化洪炉，误走了其中所封印的岁阳妖火。此事倒是可以作为旁证。
t_en: Just as {NICKNAME} said, when the Ambrosial Arbor resurrected, its roots broke through the Creation Furnace on the Luofu, accidentally releasing the heliobus fiendfire sealed inside. This can be used as circumstantial evidence.

t_ch: 炎老认可，我自然没有问题。
t_en: If Elder Huaiyan accepts the explanation, so do I.

t_ch: 幻胧渴望建木之力
t_en: Phantylia was eager to obtain the power of Ambrosial Arbor.

t_ch: 我跟随景元将军一同与幻胧交锋。她汲取了建木的力量，生成了一具难以消灭的肉体。也许打从一开始，她就是为此而来。
t_en: I was there, fighting Phantylia alongside General Jing Yuan, but she absorbed the power of the Ambrosial Arbor and gained an almost indestructible physical form. Perhaps that was her intention all along.

t_ch: 毁灭的卒子渴望一具肉身活下去？这倒是个未曾考虑的角度。有意思。
t_en: So, a pawn of the Destruction wanted a flesh-and-blood body to live in? Hmm, interesting. That's quite a new perspective.

t_ch: 拒绝回答
t_en: Refuse to answer anything.

t_ch: 我不知道该怎么回答将军的问题。
t_en: I'm not sure how to answer that question.

t_ch: 这名绝灭大君擅长操弄局势，布计令对手自灭。如今飞霄将军对罗浮内部的怀疑，恐怕也是——
t_en: The Lord Ravager is a master manipulator, adept at setting traps that lead her enemies to ruin. The current suspicions of General Feixiao towards the internal affairs on the Luofu are likely also...

t_ch: 照你的话讲，我是连问都不能问了。问就是内斗，这托词倒是和来无影去无踪的幻胧一样好用。
t_en: So, I'm not even allowed to ask questions? Asking questions is considered sowing discord now? Well, that's convenient for you, just like Phantylia vanishing without a trace.

t_ch: 请将军允许我们重新回答您的问题。
t_en: Could you please give us another chance? I'd like to answer that question again.

t_ch: 不妨事，但也要记得，辩驳亦如战场，越是犹豫，便越是远离胜利。
t_en: No problem, but remember that a debate is like a duel. The more you hesitate, the farther you are from victory.

t_ch: 你打定主意不对这个问题发表意见。
t_en: You've decided to withhold your opinion on this question.

t_ch: 飞霄微微一笑，也不动怒。
t_en: Feixiao offers a slight smile, displaying no anger.

t_ch: 你沉默地盯着天击将军，嘴角勾起一丝挑衅的笑意。
t_en: You silently fix your gaze on the Merlin's Claw, a trace of a provocative smile playing at the corners of your mouth.

t_ch: 飞霄微微一笑，也不动怒。
t_en: Feixiao offers a slight smile, displaying no anger.

t_ch: 很好，从各位的回答来看，这些问题都能有所解释。
t_en: Well, it seems that your answers have addressed all my questions.

t_ch: 看来关于无名客如何涉入这场建木灾异的细节…各位仍有一些无法自圆其说的地方啊。
t_en: Well, it appears that you are still unable to give explanations for several details about how the Nameless got involved in the Ambrosial Arbor crisis.

t_ch: 两位将军，我的问话就到此为止了。
t_en: Generals, I am finished with my questioning.

t_ch: 那么，飞霄将军觉得如何？报告中的诸多疑点，是否得到了解释？
t_en: So, what do you think, General Feixiao? Have the doubts in the report been cleared up?

t_ch: 两位无名客的回答倒是颇为坦诚，就算其中有些难以解释的细节，但以我的直觉看来，倒也没什么不妥。
t_en: The two Nameless have been honest in their answers. Even though there are some tricky details, my intuition tells me there is nothing wrong.

t_ch: 不过我方才所提到的三个问题，不仅仅是在向这两位无名客发问，也是在向景元将军传递某种声音——
t_en: However, the three questions I posed earlier were not just for the Nameless, but for you too, General Jing Yuan.

t_ch: 其一，药王秘传在罗浮内部不断壮大，六御却无所察觉，任其滋长，是为「失职」。
t_en: First, the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus grew uninterrupted on the Luofu, yet the Six Charioteers were not aware of it. That was a dereliction of duty.

t_ch: 其二，对星核猎手的说辞信之不疑，又将解决危机的重责交托外人，任其触及寿瘟祸迹，是为「失责」。
t_en: Second, you believed in the Stellaron Hunters' prophecy and entrusted outsiders to solve the crisis, even granting them access to the plaguemark. That was a dereliction of responsibility.

t_ch: 其三，于建木灾异之后，一意举行演武仪典，将罗浮再度置于寰宇焦点，是为「失智」。
t_en: Third, you insist on holding the Wardance right after the Ambrosial Arbor Crisis, putting the Luofu back in the spotlight. That is a dereliction of wisdom.

t_ch: 天击将军，这是你的意思…还是「十王」的意思？
t_en: Merlin's Claw, is this your line of thinking... or the Ten Lords'?

t_ch: 我想，打从进殿起我就说了，<color=#dbc291ff>我所问的未必是我认为的</color>。
t_en: From the moment I walked in, I made it clear that <color=#dbc291ff>the questions I'd ask might not reflect my actual thoughts</color>.

t_ch: …药王秘传的势力盘根错节，潜谋已久。景元失察，确有疏失。
t_en: ...The Disciples of Sanctus Medicus were deeply rooted and had been plotting for a long time. I admit it was my negligence for not noticing it earlier.

t_ch: 星核猎手的预言，我倒也未必全信。但在见招拆招之间，罗浮已从建木灾异中得以保全，可见艾利欧对未来的预言，亦有可观之处。
t_en: As for the Stellaron Hunters' prophecy, I didn't believe all of it, but in the end, the Luofu did survive the Ambrosial Arbor crisis. So, I think it's safe to say that Elio's prophecy about the future holds some merit.

t_ch: 至于演武仪典…景元岂会不知道开门揖盗的风险？不过风险亦是转机，罗浮这潭池水沉寂久了，也是时候该搅和搅和，让沉渣泛起，激浊扬清了。
t_en: And as for the Wardance... Do you think I'm unaware of the risks? However, risks can also be opportunities. The Luofu has lain low for too long. I believe it's time to stir up the dregs hidden in the depths and wash them away once and for all.

t_ch: 神策将军不愧是文化人，几句话赶得上一本仙舟成语大全了，我喜欢。
t_en: Just as I expected from our sophisticated Divine Foresight. You have a way with words. I like it.

t_ch: 但很遗憾，自报告上呈之日起，联盟内就充斥着流言和臆测。就连罗浮内部也有人参本上奏，指责将军疏怠职守，放任建木重生。
t_en: But unfortunately, ever since the report was submitted, the Alliance has been filled with rumors and speculation. Even within the Luofu, there are people accusing you of neglecting your duties, resulting in the Ambrosial Arbor's resurrection.

t_ch: 那么，飞霄将军又是怎么想的？
t_en: So, what are your thoughts on all of this, General Feixiao?

t_ch: 你我同为天将，自然都清楚坐这把交椅的难处。
t_en: As a fellow Arbiter-General, I fully understand the difficulties of this position.

t_ch: 在我看来，这些统统是蚊蝇无意义的嗡鸣。相隔星海，罗浮上所发生的事，唯有神策将军最清楚其危机和背后隐含的意义……
t_en: Personally, I think all these rumors are meaningless drivel. Across the sea of stars, the Divine Foresight knows better than anyone else what happened on the Luofu and the meaning behind it...

t_ch: …正如曜青仙舟最近所遭遇的情况。
t_en: ...Just as what happened on the Xianzhou Yaoqing recently.

t_ch: 你是说，曜青仙舟也…？
t_en: You mean, the Xianzhou Yaoqing is also...?

t_ch: 青丘军的斥候回报，步离人又开始蠢蠢欲动了。原本一盘散沙的步离猎群，开始彼此攻伐，互相吞并，结成更大的猎群。在他们的背后，有个叫「蟒古思」的东西在指引他们。
t_en: The scouts of the Verdant Knights have sent back reports that borisin are making trouble again. The borisin packs that were once divided and scattered have started swallowing each other up, forming larger and larger packs. Moreover, there's an entity named "Mangas" behind it all.

t_ch: 东西？
t_en: An "entity"?

t_ch: 斥候送来的情报说，那东西…不是步离人。那是一个自诩「长生主使者」的女人，「十二重面目，十二对獠牙，残酷如猛毒，变化如流沙」。步离人相信她将为他们带来重新崛起的机会。
t_en: According to the report, this entity... isn't actually a borisin. It's a woman claiming to be the Messenger of the Master of Immortality. She's described as having "twelve faces and twelve pairs of fangs, as cruel as poison and as elusive as quicksand." The borisin believe she'll give them a chance to rise again.

t_ch: 是幻胧。
t_en: That's Phantylia.

t_ch: 不错。
t_en: That's right.

t_ch: 幸好此行是我前来，若是换做{RUBY_B#有无}尘冥将军{RUBY_E#}或是{RUBY_B#爻光}戎韬将军{RUBY_E#}，这场问话或许就不会这么友善了。
t_en: You're lucky that I'm the one who came this time. If it were {RUBY_B#Youwu}the Patina Justice{RUBY_E#} or {RUBY_B#Yao Guang}the Seer Strategist{RUBY_E#}, this conversation might not be so friendly.

t_ch: 我向来相信直觉，自然不会怀疑各位光明磊落的侠士。但眼下罗浮面对的质疑难关也确凿无疑。因此我打算确保最关键的一点，给联盟一个交代。
t_en: I've always had faith in my instincts, so I don't doubt your good intentions. But the Alliance has its fair share of questions and doubts about the Luofu. So, my plan is to come up with an acceptable answer to satisfy the Alliance.

t_ch: 飞霄将军打算怎么做？
t_en: What's in this plan, General Feixiao?

t_ch: 景元将军心知肚明。只是你不愿做这坏人，那便由我代劳——建木一事仍需要十王司一锤定音，压服众声。
t_en: General Jing Yuan, you already know what has to be done, but since you don't want to be the bad guy, I'll take care of it for you. You need the final word from the Ten-Lords Commission to quell any doubts.

t_ch: 为此，我要委屈两位无名客前往幽囚狱一趟。
t_en: And for that, I'll have to ask the two Nameless to visit the Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 你要扣留我们？
t_en: Are you imprisoning us?

t_ch: 不是扣留。我会申请十王司「问字部」的判官以业镜台为诸位留下一份详细证言，补上报告所缺，也是为了堵上联盟内抗议的嘴巴。
t_en: No, I'm not imprisoning you. While you're there, I'll ask a judge in the Ten-Lords Commission's Interrogation Division to record a detailed testimony with the Karmic Mirror from both of you. We'll fill in the gaps that weren't covered in the report and silence any protests within the Alliance.

t_ch: …我没有问题。
t_en: ...I'm okay with that.

t_ch: 我也一样。
t_en: Me too.

t_ch: 好！年轻人心肠滚热，如此体贴，老朽感动得眼泪都掉下来了。
t_en: Your willingness to help is truly heartwarming, youngsters.

t_ch: 我有异议！
t_en: I object!

t_ch: 你担心我出尔反尔，将你们扣在幽囚狱里？那大可不必…我要是想做早就做了。一旦完成证言，诸位来去自由，礼遇如常。
t_en: Worried that I might go back on my word and keep you in The Shackling Prison? Relax, if I wanted to do that, you'd already be behind bars. Once you're done with your testimony, you're free to come and go as you please.

t_ch: 还放咱们回来吗…？
t_en: Will you let us come back...?

t_ch: 你担心我出尔反尔，将你们扣在幽囚狱里？那大可不必…我要是想做早就做了。一旦完成证言，诸位来去自由，礼遇如常。
t_en: Worried that I might go back on my word and keep you in The Shackling Prison? Relax, if I wanted to do that, you'd already be behind bars. Once you're done with your testimony, you're free to come and go as you please.

t_ch: 那就依天击将军所请——
t_en: Then, as the Merlin's Claw requests...

t_ch: 此外，我来罗浮还有一桩要务。如果说十王司盖上大印的证言是为压服联盟高层里反对的声音。那么曜青仙舟狐人内部的声音要恳请景元将军听听。
t_en: Oh, there's one more thing. This testimony is for silencing the voices of opposition within the Alliance, but I would like to urge General Jing Yuan to listen to the pleas of the foxians on the Xianzhou Yaoqing.

t_ch: 天击将军是为「呼雷」而来？
t_en: So, you are here for Hoolay?

t_ch: 正是。我要带走关押在罗浮幽囚狱中的步离人巢父「呼雷」，移交曜青仙舟看管。步离人近日的动向足以证明他们将有大图谋。我们必须及早因应。
t_en: Exactly. Hoolay is locked up in the Luofu's Shackling Prison. Since he is the Brood Lord of the borisin, I want to transfer him onto the Xianzhou Yaoqing and imprison him there. The recent movements of the borisin suggest they're planning something big, so we must act preemptively.

t_ch: 呼雷是狐族世仇，由狐人镇压看守也合情合理。飞霄将军信任罗浮的处置，罗浮自然也对将军的处置报以信任。炎老认为如何？
t_en: It makes sense to have the foxians keep an eye on their arch-nemesis. Since you trust my judgment, I'll repay that trust. What do you think about all this, General Huaiyan?

t_ch: 老朽本来担心这是一场唇枪舌剑的会面。是我多心了！两位既然都做出决定，互解难题，那真是再好不过！
t_en: I was worried this would turn into a heated argument, but it seems like both of you are on the same page, solving each other's problems! I couldn't have asked for a better outcome!

t_ch: 移交呼雷一事，我会派遣下属椒丘、貊泽先行对其囚牢状况进行探视，以便后续展开押解。若无其他疑问，这就开始吧？
t_en: And as for Hoolay, I'll send my lieutenants Jiaoqiu and Moze to check on his condition in prison and ready him for transport. If there are no more questions, shall we get this started?

t_ch: 实在抱歉，{NICKNAME}。幽囚狱中的笔录不会占用你们太长时间。
t_en: I'm really sorry about all this, {NICKNAME}, but the testimony in the Shackling Prison won't take up much of your time.

t_ch: 我会召来接引者，带两位送往十王司问字部判官处，两位只需留下证言即可返回，不必担忧。
t_en: I'll call for a messenger to take you to the Interrogation Division. You just need to give your testimony and then you can head back. So, no need to worry.

t_ch: 就依将军所言。
t_en: As you wish, General.

t_ch: 这就出发吧。
t_en: Let's get going.

t_ch: 能再等等吗？
t_en: Maybe just one moment?

t_ch: 化干戈为玉帛，确是好事。
t_en: Well, I'm glad we managed to talk this out.

t_ch: 飞霄打量了你一眼，尽管没有任何理由，你仍然感到她以看到对手的眼光对你上下打量。
t_en: Feixiao gives you a once-over, and despite lacking any apparent reason, you feel scrutinized, as if she were sizing up an opponent.

t_ch: 你相信星核猎手的预言？
t_en: Do you actually believe in the Stellaron Hunters' prophecy?

t_ch: 我相信
t_en: I do.

t_ch: 我相信。所以我来了。
t_en: Yes, I do. That's why I'm here.

t_ch: 我不相信
t_en: No, I don't.

t_ch: 我不相信。所以我来了，为了证明他看到的命运不存在。
t_en: No, I don't. That's why I'm here, to prove that the prophecy is not real.

t_ch: 我不知道
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 仙舟的穷观阵也能预卜未来…你相信它预言的未来吗？
t_en: The Xianzhou's Matrix of Prescience can also predict the future... Do you believe in its prophecies?

t_ch: …我明白了。
t_en: ...I understand.

t_ch: 你真的相信我们吗？
t_en: Do you really believe us?

t_ch: 飞霄点了点头。
t_en: Feixiao gives a nod.

t_ch: 语言可以掩饰，但你说话时的目光，呼吸节奏，还有气息都在代你开口。
t_en: While words can be deceiving, your gaze, your breathing, and your scent... they reveal more than your words do.

t_ch: 比起别人说什么，我更相信自己的直觉。
t_en: I trust my intuition more than any words.

t_ch: 你在看什么？
t_en: What are you looking at?

t_ch: 你果然像怀炎说的那般，是个特别的存在。
t_en: You're definitely a unique individual, just like Huaiyan said.

t_ch: 但愿你行在与帝弓为友的道路上。
t_en: I hope you'll walk alongside the Reignbow's path as a friend.

t_ch: 一段时间后，一艘星槎将你和丹恒载至一处看起来颇为熟悉的地方……
t_en: Some time later, a starskiff carries you and Dan Heng to a place that looks strikingly familiar...

t_ch: 奉景元将军呼召，我等开启入口，备妥文书，在此接引。问字部判官也已知晓两位来意。
t_en: As General Jing Yuan requested, everything is prepared for your arrival, and I am here to receive you. The judges at the Interrogation Division also know your purpose in coming.

t_ch: 幽囚狱…时隔多年，没想到再次回到了这里。
t_en: The Shackling Prison... I didn't expect to be back here after all these years.

t_ch: 丹恒……
t_en: Dan Heng...

t_ch: 不必担心我。
t_en: Don't worry about me.

t_ch: 若两位已经准备好了，我便打开狱门。
t_en: If you're ready, I'll open the gate for you.

t_ch: 这儿是幽囚狱的入口？
t_en: This is the entrance to The Shackling Prison?

t_ch: 此处是幽囚狱众多临时便于出入的穴门之一。两位并非犯人，自不能循押解犯人的门入内。客人，请入内。
t_en: This is one of many entrances used for temporary access. Since you're not actual prisoners, it makes sense for you to use this entrance. Please come on in, dear guests.

t_ch: 等等，幽囚狱也在鳞渊境里？
t_en: Wait, is The Shackling Prison inside the Scalegorge Waterscape, too?

t_ch: 严格说来，幽囚狱同处于多个洞天之中，古海不过是与其交叠的一处。客人，请入内。
t_en: Well, technically, The Shackling Prison is spread out across multiple delves, and the ancient sea is just where they overlap. Please come on in, dear guests.

t_ch: 踏进去，里面便是幽囚狱？
t_en: Once I step through that door, I'll be inside the prison?

t_ch: 正是。客人，请入内。
t_en: Exactly. Please come on in, dear guests.

t_ch: 我们准备好了，有劳。
t_en: We're ready to go in.

t_ch: 请再等等。
t_en: Just one moment, please.

t_ch: 这名武弁似乎十分清楚你是谁与你的来意。她侧身让路，示意你踏入幽囚狱的入口。
t_en: The warden seems to recognize you and understand your purpose of visit. She steps aside and motions for you to enter The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 若两位已经准备好了，可随我前往幽囚狱。
t_en: If both of you are ready, please follow me to The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 若两位已经准备好了，我便打开狱门。
t_en: If you're ready, I'll open the gate for you.

t_ch: 我们准备好了，有劳。
t_en: We're ready to go in.

t_ch: 请再等等。
t_en: Just one moment, please.

t_ch: 关押呼雷的牢笼之门，如有实质的虚空黑暗中透出令人胆寒的死寂气息。
t_en: In the suffocating, empty darkness, an air of deathly stillness lingered about the gates of the cage that once confined Hoolay, chilling all who gaze upon it to the marrow.

t_ch: 它本该是个稳妥的关押之处，可惜即便是这样的牢笼也没能困住恶兽。
t_en: The cage should have been virtually impossible to break out of, yet even it had failed to trap the savage beast for long.

t_ch: 你在看什么？
t_en: What are you looking at?

t_ch: 罗浮的幽囚狱…与曜青截然不同，竟是被压在水下的。
t_en: The Shackling Prison on the Luofu... is completely different from the one on the Yaoqing. It's completely underwater.

t_ch: 不管是在天上还是水底，要逃出去都易如反掌。
t_en: Whether it's in the clouds or underwater, breaking free would still be a piece of cake for me.

t_ch: 小子，越狱的老毛病又犯了？别打量了，你现在已是自由之身。万一教判官们发现你有什么不轨企图，高低把你抓进去关个几百年。
t_en: You're still thinking about your old jailbreak tricks, huh? Forget it. You're free now. Just don't do anything stupid, or the judges will throw you back in there and lock you up for a few hundred years.

t_ch: 用不了几天，你就会再见到我的。
t_en: You'll see me again in just a few days.

t_ch: 押解呼雷返回曜青，此事不仅对曜青狐人关系重大，对将军也极为重要，留神了。
t_en: Taking Hoolay back to the Yaoqing means a lot to the foxians on the ship and to the General herself. So, stay alert.

t_ch: 两位，吾名唤雪衣，奉十王司锁字部通令在此候命。
t_en: Guests, my name is Xueyi, and I'm here on orders from the Incarceration Division of the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 椒丘、貊泽奉天击将军飞霄之命，前来引渡步离人重犯「呼雷」前往曜青。
t_en: We're Jiaoqiu and Moze. General Feixiao sent us to extradite the borisin criminal Hoolay to the Yaoqing.

t_ch: 此次前来是为探视其关押状况，为后续交接押送事宜预先准备。判官大人想必已收到了知会。
t_en: We're here to inspect the conditions of his imprisonment and make preparations for the handover and transportation. I assume you've been briefed, your honor.

t_ch: 探视的申请已有批复。吾将是此行的引路人。
t_en: Your visit request has been approved. I'll be your guide for this trip.

t_ch: 罪囚呼雷，乃丰饶孽物步离人之战首、巢父，狐人之大敌。其人需为先后两千一百二十三场侵略战争及其连带罪行负责。
t_en: Prisoner Hoolay, the Warhead and Brood Lord of the borisin Abominations of Abundance and the arch-nemesis of the foxians, is responsible for <unbreak>2,123</unbreak> wars of aggression and countless associated crimes.

t_ch: 以其凶残暴戾，所造杀孽不可胜纪，判入幽囚狱底，受无间剑树之刑直至天地荒灭，永无宽赦……
t_en: Due to his heinous acts, he has been imprisoned in the depths of The Shackling Prison and subjected to the punishment of the Forest of Swords until the end of time. He shall never be pardoned...

t_ch: 判官大人，这判书上的文字就不必念了吧。呼雷其人是我狐族的天敌宿仇，他干下的暴行，可是曜青父母拿来止小儿夜啼的素材，每一桩我都清清楚楚。咱们快进到下一步吧。
t_en: No need to repeat his crimes and sentence, your honor. He is the greatest enemy of us foxians. The stories of his atrocities are used to terrify our children. I'm well aware of every crime he's committed. Let's move on to the next step.

t_ch: 探视重犯，每条规矩都关乎汝等安危。
t_en: When it comes to visiting criminals, there are rules in place to ensure your safety.

t_ch: 就算两位曜青使者自幼耳闻呼雷的传说，但对他的所知依旧少得可怜。唯有十王司判官才清楚，幽囚狱底下关押的到底是一头什么样的孽物。
t_en: I know you've heard legends about Hoolay since you were children, but your knowledge about him is likely very limited. Only the judges of the Ten-Lords Commission truly know what kind of abomination is locked up at the bottom of The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 自罗浮前任剑首镜流俘获呼雷以来，数百年过去了，幽囚狱从未允许呼雷进食给养。但即便馁饿至今，他依旧活着。这和记录在案的步离人生理完全不符。
t_en: It has been centuries since Jingliu, the former Sword Champion of the Luofu, captured Hoolay. And during all those years, we never provided him with any food. Yet he's somehow managed to stay alive. It defies all the documented physical characteristics of the borisin.

t_ch: 刑字部所造的无间剑树，本是对罪行累累的孽物施加痛苦业报的处决刑具。大部分步离人在受剑树刑后熬不过三日。但这妖物不同，剑片穿刺，即刻复原。受刑至今，他也依旧活着。
t_en: The Forest of Swords, forged by the Punishment Division, is a device of intense torment used to execute sinful abominations. Most borisin die within three days in the Forest, but Hoolay is different. Every time the blades pierce him, his body instantly heals. Despite the brutal punishment, he somehow manages to survive.

t_ch: 这头恶兽的种种妖异之处，便是所有规矩存在的意义。话说到这儿，两位明白了吗？
t_en: The complex rules are there because of his abnormal characteristics. Do you understand now?

t_ch: 是椒丘冒犯了…请大人继续。
t_en: I apologize for any offense caused... Please continue, your honor.

t_ch: 关于呼雷的探视禁忌事宜，吾已交付二位，<color=#dbc291ff>请认真阅读</color>。
t_en: I've given you the instructions regarding Hoolay's visitation. Please make sure you <color=#dbc291ff>read them carefully</color>.

t_ch: 还有，去往下一层之前，务必要吃下这丸丹药。
t_en: And please take this pellet before proceeding.

t_ch: 来历不明的药，我是不会吃的。
t_en: No, I'm not taking random medicine.

t_ch: 那汝等就无法进入底层探视。
t_en: Then you won't be allowed to visit Hoolay.

t_ch: 吃了吧。呼雷这厮和所有步离人一样，能释放名唤「狼毒」的信息素，唤起内心的恐惧。
t_en: Just swallow it already. Hoolay is like all borisin, he can release a pheromone called Lupitoxin that induces fear.

t_ch: 数千年前，我族受步离人长久奴役，无力反抗。不是因为狐人天然低他们一等，原因就在这个。判官大人的药可是为咱们俩的心理健康着想呢。
t_en: Thousands of years ago, we foxians were enslaved by the borisin, not because we were naturally weaker, but because of their Lupitoxin. This pill is for our own mental well-being.

t_ch: 明白了。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 我就知道你是个通情达理的人。药我们已咽下了，走吧。
t_en: I knew you were a reasonable person. Now that we've taken the medicine, let's proceed.

t_ch: 判官大人……
t_en: Your honor...

t_ch: 怎么？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 不，没事，也许是我的错觉…咱们走吧。
t_en: No, never mind, maybe I'm just imagining things... Let's keep moving.

t_ch: 我们到了。
t_en: Here we are.

t_ch: 判官尚未抵达，两位请稍作等待。
t_en: Her Honor hasn't arrived yet. Please wait a moment.

t_ch: 这里…还是和过去一样冷。
t_en: This place... it's still as cold as it used to be.

t_ch: 你还好吗，丹恒？
t_en: Are you okay, Dan Heng?

t_ch: 抱歉，记起了一些不快的回忆。
t_en: Sorry, I was just reminiscing about some unpleasant memories.

t_ch: 等完成证言，我们就离开仙舟。
t_en: Once we're done with the testimony, we'll leave immediately.

t_ch: 就算没法逃离过去，你有我们。
t_en: Even if you can't escape your past, we're always here for you.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。都是因为我的缘故，才会将你们卷入仙舟一系列事件中。你本不应该看到幽囚狱里的景色…真是抱歉。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. It's all my fault that you were involved in the Xianzhou's problems. You shouldn't have to see the inside of The Shackling Prison... I'm so sorry.

t_ch: 要是有机会，我希望能以更好的方式带你们游历罗浮的景色。
t_en: I wish I could show you the real sights of the Luofu.

t_ch: 丹恒轻轻拍了拍你的手。
t_en: Dan Heng gently pats your hand.

t_ch: 为什么不在幽囚狱外问话？
t_en: Why can't they record our testimony outside The Shackling Prison?

t_ch: 在仙舟上的其他地方，也许六御更有权威。但在幽囚狱里，十王司的判官却是至高无上的。
t_en: The Six Charioteers might have more power over the rest of this Xianzhou ship, but here, in The Shackling Prison, it's the judges of the Ten-Lords Commission who call the shots.

t_ch: 判官折狱，会借助「业镜台」之类的复杂审讯系统。那东西会在每一次问答时，如同透镜般解读受审者身体的变化与信号，留下存证。
t_en: The judges will use a complicated interrogation device called the Karmic Mirror. It accurately tracks every physiological change during questioning and records it.

t_ch: 经由此处做出的审断，足以令其他仙舟的管理者信服…至少他们不会妄加推翻。这也是飞霄执意需要我们来此的缘故吧。
t_en: Therefore, the judgment made here will be sufficient to convince the other administrators of the Xianzhou... At least they won't attempt to challenge the conclusions. Perhaps that's why Feixiao insisted that we come here.

t_ch: 判官没来，我到处逛逛。
t_en: Since the judge isn't here yet, I'll just wander around a bit.

t_ch: 好，但是别乱跑，这儿不比别的地方。
t_en: Okay, but watch your step. This place is not like the others.

t_ch: 列车的客人，久等了。问字部判官寒鸦见过两位。
t_en: Welcome, dear guests from the Express. Judge Hanya of the Interrogation Division, we've met before.

t_ch: 寒鸦小姐？
t_en: Miss Hanya?

t_ch: 又见面了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Glad to meet you again, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 怎么是你？
t_en: It's you!?

t_ch: 寒鸦身为问字部判官，将会接手此次证言记录。
t_en: As one of the judges from the Interrogation Division, I'll be recording your testimony today.

t_ch: 判官就你们姐妹俩是吧？
t_en: Are you and your sister the only judges here?

t_ch: 怎么，{NICKNAME}不想见到我？也罢，我可以唤来擅用针灸的判官，精通斧锯的判官，又或是长于鞭索的判官来问话…多种选择，必有一款适合您。
t_en: Hmm? You don't want to see me, {NICKNAME}? Well, in that case, I can call in the judge who's an expert in acupuncture, or the one who's handy with axes and saws, or the one who knows all about whips and ropes... We've got plenty of options here, and we'll find one that suits your taste.

t_ch: 开个小玩笑。判官执勤轮流周转，{NICKNAME}只是刚巧碰上了我，可见我俩冥冥中有些缘分。
t_en: Just kidding. We judges work in shifts, and it just happens to be my shift, {NICKNAME}. Maybe we're destined to cross paths.

t_ch: 此前{RUBY_B#镇伏岁阳}绥园伏魔{RUBY_E#}一事，真是有劳您奔波了，容寒鸦再度致谢。
t_en: Please allow me to express my gratitude to you again for {RUBY_B#Subduing Heliobi}subduing the demons in the Fyxestroll Garden{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 看来在我和三月七不知不觉的时候，你已经在罗浮仙舟上颇有人缘了啊。
t_en: Looks like while March 7th and I were clueless, you already made many friends on the Xianzhou Luofu.

t_ch: 都是机缘巧合，机缘巧合。
t_en: It was all a lucky coincidence.

t_ch: 毕竟在家靠帕姆，出外靠朋友。
t_en: Pom-Pom takes care of us at home, and friends take care of us on the road. That's just how it goes.

t_ch: 虽说{NICKNAME}与我有一面之缘，但十王御下，不容徇私。前往「录事厅」留下证言以及之后的一切流程，请两位务必听我号令，不得擅自行动。
t_en: Even though {NICKNAME} and I have met before, we can't show any favoritism under the Ten Lords. So, please do as I command as we head to Scrivener Hall and beyond. Don't do anything without my permission.

t_ch: 此处不是阳世凡人久待之地。丹恒先生，{NICKNAME}，随我来。
t_en: This is not a place for ordinary mortals. {NICKNAME} and Mr. Dan Heng, please come with me.

t_ch: 请判官为我们引路。
t_en: Please lead the way, your honor.

t_ch: 待我启动机关，各位再向前走，务必小心脚下。
t_en: Please let me activate the mechanism before we all move forward, and please watch your steps.

t_ch: 这一路行去，各位一定要留神自己的落脚处。
t_en: Be very careful and watch your step as you make your way through.

t_ch: 幽囚狱内地势险怪，稍有不慎便会坠入深处。运气要是背些，只怕在救援赶到前，会被冻入幽邃坚冰里，又或是被焦炎烧成炭烬。
t_en: The terrain inside the Shackling Prison is treacherous. One wrong move, and you could end up plunging into the depths. And if you're really unlucky, you might be frozen solid or burned to a crisp before anyone can help you.
Gee...

t_ch: 不过以两位的身手我想不必担心。
t_en: But with your skills, I don't think you need to worry about that.

t_ch: 这儿不会有十八层吧？
t_en: So, are there really eighteen floors here?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}说笑了，幽囚狱各处山重水复，层层相叠。还请来了持明的龙脉巧匠，用尽了洞天之术的奇巧。两位肉眼所见的只是幽囚狱的一部分。
t_en: You're funny, {NICKNAME}. This prison does contain many layers. We even brought in Vidyadhara Ichor Line craftsmen to introduce the ingenuity of delve enchantment. What you're seeing now is just one part of The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 真的吗，丹恒？
t_en: Is that true, Dan Heng?

t_ch: 也许吧，从外观看，这座幽囚狱的许多建筑都留有持明的制式…也许在古早之前，持明确实参与营建了此处。
t_en: Perhaps. Judging from the appearance, much of the architecture in this prison has a Vidyadharan style... So perhaps they did have a hand in building this place back then.

t_ch: 要问此处到底有多少层？让两位见笑了，即便身为判官的我也不知道。
t_en: As for how many floors there are...? Well, not even us judges know for sure.

t_ch: 不过判官之间流传，有短生种犯人试图逃脱，却不慎坠下…当我们寻到他时，他已是个垂垂老者了……
t_en: But there's a legend among us judges that a short-life prisoner once tried to escape but ended up falling into the depths... and when we found him at the bottom, he was already an old man on the brink of death...

t_ch: 如此想来，他究竟是在下落中度过了一生的时间，还是被某些长生种犯人吸食精气变老？这就不得而知了。总之，两位可不要轻易尝试啊。
t_en: Did he spend his whole life falling? Or did some long-life prisoner drain his life essence? We'll never know. Anyway, please don't try anything like that.

t_ch: 怎么不好好施工？
t_en: Why keep the terrain so dangerous?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}说笑了，幽囚狱各处山重水复，层层相叠。还请来了持明的龙脉巧匠，用尽了洞天之术的奇巧。两位肉眼所见的只是幽囚狱的一部分。
t_en: You're funny, {NICKNAME}. This prison does contain many layers. We even brought in Vidyadhara Ichor Line craftsmen to introduce the ingenuity of delve enchantment. What you're seeing now is just one part of The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 真的吗，丹恒？
t_en: Is that true, Dan Heng?

t_ch: 也许吧，从外观看，这座幽囚狱的许多建筑都留有持明的制式…也许在古早之前，持明确实参与营建了此处。
t_en: Perhaps. Judging from the appearance, much of the architecture in this prison has a Vidyadharan style... So perhaps they did have a hand in building this place back then.

t_ch: 只不过幽囚狱的路既是为十王司人员所设的通道，也是为犯人所设的障碍。
t_en: Well, the paths here serve as both passageways for the Ten-Lords Commission and barriers for the prisoners.

t_ch: 此地关押的多是些大奸大恶，触犯仙舟律条的犯人。这班人中又以步离、慧骃、{RUBY_B#造翼者}羽夷{RUBY_E#}之类的丰饶孽物居多。这些丰饶民，说句不妥帖的，各个身怀绝技…谋划脱狱之事时有发生。
t_en: Most prisoners locked up here have committed serious crimes violating the Xianzhou Legal Code, and many of them are Denizens of Abundance like borisin, houyhnhnm, and {RUBY_B#featherines}wingweavers{RUBY_E#}. To be honest, these abominations... are pretty good at trying to break out.

t_ch: 只消能为他们逃离此处多增添一份困难，这些艰难道路对咱们十王司的人来说算得了什么。
t_en: So, as long as the treacherous terrain here makes it harder for them to escape, the Ten-Lords Commission doesn't have any complaints.

t_ch: 两位这边走。
t_en: Please come this way.

t_ch: 前往阴寒狱层
t_en: Go to the Grimfrost Hold Floor

t_ch: 要去录事厅，须经由此处悬梯，通过寒冰狱。
t_en: To reach Scrivener Hall, we must take this elevator and go through the Frozen Prison.

t_ch: 那里阴寒透体，请稍作忍耐。
t_en: Please brace yourself for the biting cold.

t_ch: 前往中转狱层
t_en: Go to the Transitory Hold floor

t_ch: 前往焦热狱层
t_en: Go to the Pyroscape Hold floor

t_ch: 听闻阳世即将召开演武仪典。寒鸦犹记得自己小时候曾与姐姐观赏过其中一场剑斗。
t_en: I heard the Wardance is about to be held in the mortal world. I remember watching a sword duel at the Wardance with my sister when we were young.

t_ch: 为罗浮守擂的云骑剑士英武非凡，剑光如电。异邦的挑战者一头火发，一只手竟是镔铁铸成，拳锋如雷。两人相斗良久，难分胜负。
t_en: The Cloud Knight who was the Luofu's ringmaster was an amazing swordsman, and their sword flashed like lightning. The outworld challenger had fiery red hair and a hand made of steel which roared like thunder. They battled for a long time before a winner could be declared.

t_ch: 一眨眼，时间已经过去了这么久。寒鸦和姐姐都成了冥世之身，再不会为什么比赛激动雀跃了。
t_en: Time sure flies. Now, both my sister and I are residents of the netherworld, and we will never again feel the thrill of a competition.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，这一回演武仪典，星槎海的人流多吗？
t_en: Are there a lot of people in Starskiff Haven due to the Wardance, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 熙熙攘攘，看不到边。
t_en: Yeah, it's bustling.

t_ch: 真好啊。可惜寒鸦公务繁忙，不能观赏赛事…以我如今的样貌，去了只怕也会吓到旁人。
t_en: Sounds amazing. It's a shame I'm too busy to attend... but even if I could, I'd probably just scare people off with my current appearance.

t_ch: 咱们不上电梯吗？
t_en: Aren't we going to take the elevator?

t_ch: 对不起，是我走神了。
t_en: Sorry, I got a bit distracted there.

t_ch: 咱们走吧。
t_en: Let's get going, then.

t_ch: …好冷。
t_en: ...It's freezing.

t_ch: 此处引来极境洞天北号峰的寒气。
t_en: This place is filled with the cold air from the Northern Peak of the Polar Delve.

t_ch: 饶是生命力顽强的长生种也抵受不住。
t_en: Even the toughest long-life species would have a hard time enduring this.

t_ch: 箱笼堆叠，真是碍眼。
t_en: Ugh, what an eyesore. This place is packed with boxes and crates.

t_ch: 嗯？两位听见什么响动了吗？
t_en: Hmm? Did you hear something just now?

t_ch: 让我看看怎么回事。
t_en: Let me check it out.

t_ch: 这些箱子…看起来有些眼熟。
t_en: These crates... they look oddly familiar.

t_ch: 前些日子冥差收到传唤，说是公司的运输船遭受了步离人袭击。随后一大群孽畜便被丢进了这里。
t_en: A few days ago, the spiritfarers received reports about an IPC transport ship that was attacked by borisin. Then, a bunch of those abominations were dumped into this place.

t_ch: 想来演武仪典召开，总有不轨之徒蠢蠢欲动。只是货物与劫匪一同被扔进监狱的事情，我倒是从没见过。
t_en: I had a feeling there would be trouble during the Wardance. But throwing both the pirates and the cargo in jail? That's a new one.

t_ch: 那些箱子里的机甲可不好惹。
t_en: The mechs in those crates are no joke.

t_ch: 公司似乎招惹了步离人…
t_en: Looks like the IPC has caught the attention of the borisin...

t_ch: 我听说啦，博识学会的人似乎造出了某种危险的东西。因果殿的卷宗中有不少关于这些智叟的记录，他们为了突破机巧技术的极限，总喜欢涉足禁忌研究。
t_en: I heard the Intelligentsia Guild crafted something dangerous. We have many records in the Hall of Karma about these wise ones. They love to tinker with forbidden technologies, always trying to push the limits of ingenia.

t_ch: 我见过箱中的机甲，造型与步离人颇为近似。真不知道这回他们打算做什么？
t_en: I caught a glimpse of the mechs in those crates, and they bear a striking resemblance to borisin. I wonder what they're planning this time?

t_ch: 正事要紧，我们走吧。
t_en: Well, business first. Let's keep moving.

t_ch: 怎么回事？这些东西为何动起来了？
t_en: What's happening here? Why are these things moving on their own?

t_ch: 不对劲，守卫们毫无回应…
t_en: Something is wrong. The guards aren't responding...

t_ch: 这东西…发起狂来和步离人一样。
t_en: This thing... is going wild, just like a borisin.

t_ch: 看来，我们得自救了。
t_en: Looks like we're on our own for this one.

t_ch: 这怎么可能，冥差们已经按照规程切断了箱子里机器的动力，还在箱子之外施加了封印。为何这些机甲还能行动？
t_en: But how is this even possible? The spiritfarers followed the protocols, cutting off power to the mechs and sealing the crates. How could these mechs still start moving?

t_ch: 和当时在工造司里的情况一样，这些货物仿佛失去了控制一般攻击在场所有人。
t_en: It's just like what happened in the Artisanship Commission before. These goods went haywire and attacked everyone in sight.

t_ch: 灵砂说机甲内有生物组织。
t_en: Lingsha mentioned there was some kind of biological tissue inside the mechs.

t_ch: 丹鼎司的人检查过机甲，这东西内部隐含着非同一般的结构。
t_en: The Alchemy Commission members examined them and found some unusual structures within.

t_ch: 冥差呢？为什么没人增援？
t_en: Where are the spiritfarers? Why hasn't anyone come to help us?

t_ch: 从刚才开始，似乎有某种东西在干扰幽囚狱内的通讯。
t_en: Something seems to be jamming communications within The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 说不定正是这些机甲干的好事。
t_en: It's probably these mechs causing the trouble.

t_ch: 这样危险的东西出现在幽囚狱里，唯一的目的就是——
t_en: These things showing up in The Shackling Prison can only mean one thing...

t_ch: 劫狱。
t_en: A prison break.

t_ch: 而送来这些「货物」的人，显然希望仙舟严格的审核流程能将它的危险暴露在天舶司和云骑的眼前。他们想确保的是它一定会被送入幽囚狱。
t_en: And whoever delivered these "goods" clearly wanted them to go through the Xianzhou's strict inspection process, to show the Sky-Faring Commission and Cloud Knights how dangerous they were. They wanted these mechs to end up right here in The Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 如果在所有人无法察觉的时候，这些东西已经开始行动。那整座监狱现在的状况就非常危险了。
t_en: If these things already started taking action while nobody was paying attention, then the whole prison is in trouble, I'm afraid.

t_ch: 更危险的是，除两位之外，刚才还有一队来访者进入了幽囚狱深处——曜青仙舟的使者。
t_en: And to make things worse, another group of visitors just entered the depths of The Shackling Prison, the messengers from the Xianzhou Yaoqing.

t_ch: 他们探访的对象…也许就是这些狼形机甲被送入此地的目标。若是让镇压在底部的那头恶兽逃了出来…恐怕罗浮仙舟也将有大灾难！
t_en: And the prisoner they came to visit... might be the target whom these wolf-shaped mechs were delivered here for. If that vicious beast manages to break free... it will be a catastrophe for the Xianzhou Luofu!

t_ch: 片刻之前，雪衣已引领曜青使者来到幽狱之底。
t_en: A few moments prior, Xueyi leads the Yaoqing messengers to the bottom of the prison.

t_ch: 到了。
t_en: Here we are.

t_ch: 判官大人，这便到了吗？
t_en: Have we arrived already, your honor?

t_ch: 这儿可没什么囚笼啊？
t_en: Shouldn't there be a cage here?

t_ch: 犯尽十恶，大逆不道的罪人都被镇于幽狱之底，以单独的洞天囚禁。若无谕令，等闲不会开启。
t_en: The most notorious felons are locked away in solitary delves deep down in the prison. Those delves can't be opened without proper authorization.

t_ch: 「青鸟开道，冥灯照路」。助我点亮这些灯座，通往幽狱之底的前路自会显现。
t_en: "The blue bird paves the path, and the Stygian Lanterns illuminate it." Help me light up these lanterns, and the way to the bottom of the Shackling Prison will reveal itself.

t_ch: 点灯所需的图示吾已交给你了。看看吧。
t_en: I've given you the diagrams for lighting the lanterns. Please take a look.

t_ch: 两位，路已开启。一旦下到幽狱之底，绝不要轻举妄动。
t_en: The path is open. Once we descend to the bottom of the prison, please do not do anything reckless.

t_ch: 在这扇门后的洞天里，关押的便是狐人的大敌，呼雷。
t_en: Inside the delve behind this door is the greatest enemy of the foxians, Hoolay.

t_ch: 呃啊啊啊啊啊——
t_en: Arrrrgh...

t_ch: 依循常例，曜青使节每百年一次抵达罗浮仙舟，确认这头孽物的关押事宜与其生死状况。
t_en: According to custom, envoys from the Yaoqing visit the Xianzhou Luofu once every century to check on this abomination's imprisonment and condition.

t_ch: 尽管十王司判处呼雷受无间剑树之刑，令其日日苦楚，万世不休。但吾很明白，曜青使者们最想看到的是呼雷咽气的死状……
t_en: Even though the Ten-Lords Commission sentenced Hoolay to the Forest of Swords, suffering every day for the rest of his life, I understand that the Yaoqing messengers want to see him dead...

t_ch: 可惜，这七百年来他们全都失望而归了，那是头杀不死的恶兽。
t_en: Unfortunately, for the past seven centuries, they've had to return disappointed, because this beast simply can't be killed.

t_ch: 世间至毒若能入药，换回一条无辜的生命，也或许能稍稍抵偿他所犯下的累累血罪了。
t_en: If we can use his toxin to create medicine and save an innocent life, it might help balance out some of the sins he has committed.

t_ch: 治愈将军的法子，会是你吗，呼雷？
t_en: Could you be the key to a cure for the General, Hoolay?

t_ch: 这一次，曜青的使节也将失望而归。但我和我的兄弟们就不同了……
t_en: And once again, the envoys of the Yaoqing will leave disappointed. However, I won't say the same for me and my brothers...
Who's there!?

t_ch: 在下是「犀犬猎群」一介小小的策问官，你可以叫我…末度。
t_en: I'm just a humble counselor of the Rhinohound Pack. You can call me... Mok Tok.

t_ch: 武弁！底层遭到入侵，速速增援！
t_en: Wardens! Intruders on the lowest level! Send reinforcements!

t_ch: 在幽狱之底，没人能听见诸位的呼喊。
t_en: Nobody will hear you here, at the bottom of the Shackling Prison.

t_ch: 多谢你替我们打开了囚牢，判官。剩下的事情，交给我们就好。
t_en: Thank you for opening up the prison for us, your honor. We'll take it from here.

t_ch: 难怪我始终嗅到一股熟悉的兽臭味，原来这不是我的错觉啊。貊泽，动手！
t_en: No wonder I kept smelling that familiar stench. So, it wasn't just my imagination. Do your thing, Moze!

t_ch: 两位，绝不能让这些孽物靠近！
t_en: We mustn't let these abominations get any closer!

t_ch: 可恶，这些孽物人数太多了。
t_en: Detestable things, there are too many of them.

t_ch: 一旦进入幽囚狱，可用之「兵」到处都是。
t_en: Once we were inside The Shackling Prison, we found "soldiers" available for us everywhere.

t_ch: 我奉劝诸位及早投降。
t_en: I suggest you surrender now.

t_ch: 当然，我绝不会留各位性命。
t_en: Of course, I'll still kill you all.

t_ch: …至少这样省去了无谓的痛苦。
t_en: ...But I promise it'll be swift.

t_ch: 口出狂言！受死吧！
t_en: How presumptuous! Die now!

t_ch: 貊泽，找机会离开。
t_en: Leave if you can, Moze.

t_ch: …还没到这时候。
t_en: ...It's not time yet.

t_ch: 距离上次狩猎，{RUBY_B#究竟过去了多少年}青丘究竟旋转了多少次{RUBY_E#}？{RUBY_B#步离人的血裔}都蓝的崽子{RUBY_E#}！告诉我你的名字！
t_en: {RUBY_B#how many years}How many times{RUBY_E#} has Verdantia spun around since our last hunt? Ah, {RUBY_B#scions of borisin}Duran's whelp{RUBY_E#}! Tell me your name!

t_ch: 伟大的呼雷汗，狐人之敌，众生的猎者。我只是犀犬猎群中一个小小的策问官，也许曾是您血脉中产下的最微不足道的子嗣之一。我名{RUBY_B#Mok Tok}末度{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: O, Great Hoolay, nemesis of the foxians, and the hunter of all beings, I'm Mok Tok, a humble counselor of the Rhinohound Pack. I am only one insignificant heir spawned from your bloodline.

t_ch: 距离您上次率领我族驰骋星海的猎场，已经过去…至少七百年时间了。看到您依旧如过去般狡猾机敏，我无限欢欣。
t_en: It's been... at least seven centuries since you led our Pack through the hunting grounds of the stars. I'm thrilled to see that you are as cunning and skillful as ever.

t_ch: 七百年…七百年过去了，都蓝的崽子为什么长成了你这般模样？回答我，末度，为什么你长成了我们最可鄙的奴隶和敌人——狐人的模样？
t_en: Seven centuries... Seven centuries have passed, but why have Duran's whelps grown to look like this? Tell me, Mok Tok, why have you grown to resemble our most despicable slaves and enemies, the foxians?

t_ch: 我受命前来，将您从可憎的囚牢中释放。这是{RUBY_B#命运}Sain Jiyaa {RUBY_E#}注定如此。为此，我不得不服下魔药，披上{RUBY_B#狐人}贱畜{RUBY_E#}的{RUBY_B#形貌}皮{RUBY_E#}，用伪装来应对他们的虚伪。
t_en: I've been commanded to release you from this cursed prison. It is {RUBY_B#fate}Sain Jiyaa{RUBY_E#}'s will. That's why I had to take a magic pill, don the skin of {RUBY_B#foxian}a lowly beast{RUBY_E#}, and play along with their hypocrisy.

t_ch: 既然你有逃离的计划，那么说说，接下来该怎么做才能离开这艘大到没边的天船？
t_en: Since you have an escape plan, tell me, what's our next move to get off of this ridiculously large ship?

t_ch: 我和弟兄们会封死这座监狱的门关，把狱卒闷死在里面。这样我们能暂时得到喘息的时间。
t_en: My brothers and I will lock down this prison, trapping the prison guards inside. This will buy us some time.

t_ch: 那些和我一样伪装的弟兄们会为您的逃离准备船只。按照计划，我们总共有两个时辰离开这儿。
t_en: The rest of my crew, who are undercover like me, will secure the vessels for our escape. According to the plan, we only have four hours to get out of here.

t_ch: 原本我打算在明日执行营救您的计划，不过他们打算将您送往曜青仙舟关押，我必须趁此良机，将您解放出来。
t_en: Originally, I intended to carry out the plan tomorrow, but they're planning to transfer you to the Xianzhou Yaoqing now, so I seized this opportunity to set you free.

t_ch: 有勇无谋的计划，蠢货！就算有船，我们能逃出去的机会也比狐人逃过我爪子的机会要少得多！
t_en: What a reckless plan, you idiot! Even if we manage to get the ships, our chances of escaping are slimmer than a foxian slipping through my claws!

t_ch: 为了救回您，我们别无选择！派我前来的长生主的天使如此说过，「只有您的回归才能结束步离人漫长的分裂」。
t_en: But we have no other choice! The angel serving under the Master of Immortality sent me with a message, "Only your return will end the prolonged divisions among the borisin."

t_ch: 此行的所有兄弟都已做好了牺牲的准备——只要能让您离开这儿。
t_en: Every one of my crew is ready to lay down their lives if it means setting you free.

t_ch: 像你这样靠狡诈求生的弱者，居然在计划中表现出近乎愚蠢的勇悍。你会得偿所愿的…所有都蓝的崽子都会得偿所愿的。
t_en: A sneaky weakling like you is actually showing some reckless bravery with this plan. Fine, you'll get what you want... and all of Duran's whelps will too.

t_ch: 那么，大人，我们该走了。
t_en: Well then, my Lord, we should leave now.

t_ch: 在离开前，把你们吃下的魔药给我一丸。
t_en: Before we go, give me one of those magic pills.

t_ch: 您…您居然要披上贱畜的皮？这有辱您伟大的身份……
t_en: My Lord... you seriously want to don the skin of a lowly beast? That would be a disgrace to your greatness, my Lord...

t_ch: 白痴，失去自由的伟大一文不值！一旦离开此地，我需要一张至少看起来不那么可疑的皮囊。
t_en: You idiot! Greatness means nothing without freedom! Once I get out, I'll need a disguise that won't raise any suspicion.

t_ch: 谨遵您的命令。
t_en: As you wish, my Lord.

t_ch: 那个狐人奴隶，是献给我的食粮吗？
t_en: Is that foxian slave my meal?

t_ch: 不，他是曜青仙舟的使节。请您暂时忍耐爪牙，他还有作为人质的价值。带上他，咱们走！
t_en: No, he's an envoy from the Xianzhou Yaoqing. I plead you to endure him a bit, my Lord, as he's more valuable as a hostage. Take him, guys! Time to move out!

t_ch: 与此同时，{NICKNAME}、丹恒与寒鸦察觉到了狱中的情况业已失控……
t_en: At the same time, {NICKNAME}, Dan Heng, and Hanya realize that the situation in the prison is out of control...

t_ch: 寒鸦大人，各区域武弁正在重整队伍。那些机巧敌人正在到处游走，杀伤了不少同僚。
t_en: Madam Hanya, the wardens in all areas are regrouping. Those ingenium enemies are wandering around and causing heavy casualties.

t_ch: 不止如此，那些铁狼还破坏囚笼，放走了罪犯！我们已经向外求援了，但看起来所有的通讯都被阻绝了。
t_en: And to make things worse, those iron wolves broke open the cages and let the criminals out! We've called for backup, but it seems like all communication is jammed.

t_ch: 保存实力为先，优先镇伏落单的囚犯和劫狱者。奉十王敕，凡有脱狱、劫狱者，不禁杀伤。
t_en: Let's preserve our strength and first take down the isolated prisoners. By decree of the Ten Lords, all prison breakers and intruders shall be apprehended, dead or alive.

t_ch: 一刻之前，「断狱轮钥」的机关启动。想来是雪衣大人和曜青使节进入幽狱之底进行探视了。以眼下失联的情况来看，贼人的目标正是此处。
t_en: Just a short time ago, the Nether Key mechanism was activated. It was probably Madam Xueyi and the Yaoqing messengers, heading to the bottom of the prison. Given the situation, I'm afraid that area is exactly where our enemies are targeting.

t_ch: 绝不可让那头恶兽从牢笼中脱出！
t_en: We mustn't let that vile beast escape from its cage!

t_ch: 已清点尚能战斗的狱卒，即将开赴各层搜捕。
t_en: We've gathered all the prison guards who can still fight, and we'll split up and secure each floor.

t_ch: 去吧！
t_en: Go!

t_ch: 那些劫狱者如此胆大妄为，一定为这次行动计划了许久。
t_en: Those intruders are quite bold. They must have been planning this for quite a while.

t_ch: 你说的不错。只是奇怪步离人从来更乐衷于暴力杀伤，制定如此精密周详的计划…不像他们一贯的风格。
t_en: Indeed, but borisin tend to favor direct and aggressive approaches. Crafting careful and precise plans like this... is simply not their style.

t_ch: 眼下情势危急，不是复盘审视的时候。两位，请先助我一臂之力！
t_en: Let's focus on the current situation right now. Please help me!

t_ch: 证言的事先放一边吧？
t_en: Looks like the testimony will have to wait, huh?

t_ch: 果然咱们所到之处没有太平。
t_en: Like I said, wherever we go, we always end up in trouble.

t_ch: 寒鸦小姐，最快前往幽狱之底的通路在什么地方？若不能在时间上争分夺秒，恐怕整个幽囚狱都会成为我们的敌人。
t_en: What's the fastest way to the bottom of this prison, Miss Hanya? If we don't get there quickly, we'll soon be outnumbered by all the enemies inside.

t_ch: 为今之计，只有使些取巧的办法了，诸位随我来。
t_en: We'll have to bend the rules. Please follow me.

t_ch: 奉十王敕，凡有脱狱、劫狱者，不禁杀伤！
t_en: By decree of the Ten Lords, all prison breakers and intruders shall be apprehended, dead or alive.

t_ch: 是！
t_en: Yes, ma'am!

t_ch: 守住此地。
t_en: Hold your position here.

t_ch: 遵命！
t_en: Yes, ma'am!

t_ch: 要想尽快抵达幽狱之底，必须借助某些工具。
t_en: We need a few tools if we want to reach the bottom quickly.

t_ch: 随我去下一层。
t_en: Follow me to the next floor.

t_ch: 道路断了？一定是有人更动了机关。
t_en: The path is cut off? Someone must have altered the mechanism.

t_ch: 无妨，稍加操作便能打通。
t_en: No matter, it'll just require a bit more work to get through.

t_ch: 幽囚狱中道路难行，有些冥差偶尔为了贪图脚程便捷，借用工造司里的机巧来去穿梭。
t_en: Some spiritfarers occasionally use ingenia from the Artisanship Commission to navigate the treacherous terrain here.

t_ch: 狱内本是禁止使用「换境画屏」之类的装置，以免便利了犯人逃离。
t_en: Normally, using devices like the shifting screen is strictly forbidden, as they could help prisoners escape.

t_ch: 如今事急从权，也顾不得什么命令了。
t_en: But given the urgency of the situation, let's just ignore the usual regulations.

t_ch: 太好了，这东西还在。
t_en: Good, it's still here.

t_ch: 不知它还能不能连通另一处。
t_en: I wonder if it can still take us to the other side.

t_ch: 「断狱轮钥」已打开了通路，希望我们来得还不算晚……
t_en: The Nether Key has been activated. I hope we're not too late...

t_ch: 姐姐，你要坚持住啊！
t_en: Hang in there, sister!

t_ch: 看起来就在不久前，这里经历了一场恶战。
t_en: Looks like there was a fierce battle here not long ago.

t_ch: 狱门与囚笼洞天断开了联系，还是让呼雷逃出来了。
t_en: The gate to the prison delve is wide open. Hoolay has managed to break free.

t_ch: 曜青使者呢？姐姐呢？他们还活着吗？
t_en: Where are the Yaoqing messengers, and where's my sister? Are they still alive?

t_ch: 四下找找，应该会有眉目的。
t_en: Let's look around. Maybe we'll find some clues.

t_ch: 雪衣…在这儿。
t_en: Xueyi... is here.

t_ch: 雪衣小姐壮烈成仁…寒鸦小姐，请节哀。
t_en: Miss Xueyi bravely sacrificed herself... My condolences, Miss Hanya.

t_ch: 哀悼？我没那个意思。
t_en: Condolences? Well, that's unnecessary.

t_ch: 你误会了。蒙十王恩赐，姐姐在数百年前早已成了机巧之身。死亡后其「灵魂」回归因果殿的案牍库，对她来说早已是家常便饭。
t_en: You misunderstand. Thanks to the Ten Lords' blessing, my sister turned into an ingenium centuries ago. Dying and returning as just a soul to the Hall of Karma's document office has become a routine for her.

t_ch: 虽然我也不想看到她每次如此轻率地消耗自己的身体，就像一个可以被轻率替换的零件那般。但……
t_en: While I'm not keen on seeing her throw away her body like it's some replaceable part, but...

t_ch: 对了，有没有找到可能是曜青使节的伤亡者？其中一人是衣装华美的狐人，另一个披挂暗色袍子，瞧着像是个亡命之徒。
t_en: By the way, have you seen bodies that might be the Yaoqing messengers? One of them is a foxian wearing exquisite clothes, and the other wears a dark cloak, like a fugitive.

t_ch: …不，除去步离人之外，这里的遗骸中我没瞧见长得像狐人的死者。
t_en: ...No, I haven't found any foxian bodies, only borisin ones.

t_ch: 也就是说，劫狱者和呼雷挟持了曜青使者。
t_en: So, the intruders and Hoolay are holding the Yaoqing messengers hostage.

t_ch: 这真是最糟糕的情况了。人质在手，狱卒们对这些家伙恐怕也无能为力。万一有什么闪失，罗浮仙舟与曜青仙舟的关系…恐怕也将陷入不可挽回的撕裂。
t_en: This is the worst situation. With hostages in their grasp, the prison guards can't do anything about the prisoners. If anything happens to the messengers, the relationship between the Yaoqing and the Luofu... could be irreversibly damaged.

t_ch: 必须救下使节，逃离此处，向外递送消息。只要还有一线希望，就绝不能放弃！
t_en: We must rescue the messengers, get out of here, and spread the news. We mustn't give up as long as there's a glimmer of hope!

t_ch: 寒鸦小姐发现了什么？
t_en: What have you found, Miss Hanya?

t_ch: 寒…寒……
t_en: Han... Han...

t_ch: 什么鬼！
t_en: What the heck!?

t_ch: 赛博幽灵！
t_en: A cyber specter!

t_ch: 重…重新……
t_en: Re... Reconstru...

t_ch: 是姐姐。看来她的灵魂恪尽职守，暂时还没回到因果殿中。
t_en: It's my sister. Looks like her soul hasn't returned to the Hall of Karma yet.

t_ch: 恰恰是在这节骨眼上…姐姐的「死亡」反倒成了我们的情报优势。
t_en: At such a critical moment... her "death" can give us information that we can turn into an advantage.

t_ch: 让我瞧瞧，损坏倒也不太严重。
t_en: Let me see. The damage isn't too bad.

t_ch: 若是能修好它，姐姐便有了暂时的凭寄之身。
t_en: If I can fix it up, my sister will have a temporary body.

t_ch: 寒鸦！吾吾吾…回来了！
t_en: Hanya! I—I—I'm back!

t_ch: 欢迎回来，姐姐。我不知道这时候该做什么表情好。高兴？喜极而泣？算了，我已经习惯了你用各种面目回来。
t_en: Welcome back, sister. I don't know what kind of expression I should have at a time like this. Happy? Tears of joy? Ah, forget it. I'm used to you coming back in all sorts of forms.

t_ch: 赛博幽灵！
t_en: A cyber specter!

t_ch: 只是是是权宜之计。
t_en: It's just a t-t-temporary solution.

t_ch: 好方便的能力！
t_en: That's pretty convenient!

t_ch: 切莫让吾再再再次损坏…会丢失记记记记忆。
t_en: Just don't let me be d—d—damaged again... I'll lose my m—m—memories.

t_ch: 新造型有点可爱…
t_en: Your new form is kind of cute...

t_ch: 眼下不不不是说笑的时候！
t_en: Now is not the time for j—j—jokes!

t_ch: 这东西无法承载….吾的的的全部部部……
t_en: This cycrane can't... h—h—hold all of me...

t_ch: 姐姐，在「死后」，你看到了什么？
t_en: Sister, what did you see when you were "killed"?

t_ch: 机巧鸟虽然断断续续，但简明扼要地为你们提供了情报。
t_en: Though intermittent in its delivery, the cycrane provides you with succinct and essential intelligence.

t_ch: 有一名曜青使者逃脱了？另外一人被挟持向上逃去？
t_en: One of the Yaoqing messengers escaped? And the other one was taken hostage and brought upwards?

t_ch: 除步离人外…还有…别的…入侵者…看不见……
t_en: Besides the borisin... there were... other... intruders... Invisible...

t_ch: 看不见的入侵者？这是什么意思？到底有多少阴谋掺和到这里来了？
t_en: Invisible intruders? What does that even mean? How many conspiracies are tangled up in this mess?

t_ch: 姐姐，帮我个忙，劫狱者们切断了幽囚狱对内与对外的一切联系。眼下只有你是唯一能不受阻碍逃离此处的人…呃，鸟？
t_en: Do me a favor, sister. The intruders have cut off all communication between inside and outside the prison, and now you're the only person... the only bird... who can slip out undetected.

t_ch: 务必，务必将消息递往外界。
t_en: Make sure you deliver the news to the outside world.

t_ch: 吾…明白。小妹…保重，汝和吾…毕竟不同……
t_en: I... understand. Please be careful... sister. You... are different... from me...

t_ch: 寒鸦小姐，时间紧迫，再拖延下去，步离人恐怕将会逃离监狱。
t_en: Time is running out, Miss Hanya. If we keep delaying, those borisin will escape from the prison.

t_ch: 好，若是路上能发现那位逃脱的曜青使者……
t_en: Alright. If we happen to come across that Yaoqing messenger who got away...

t_ch: 不，阻止呼雷逃离也许才是最优先的目标。
t_en: No, stopping Hoolay should probably be our top priority.

t_ch: 等等，不对劲，有什么东西在靠近我们。
t_en: Wait, something feels off. Something is approaching us.

t_ch: 看不见的入侵者？
t_en: The invisible intruders?

t_ch: 诸位，小心！
t_en: Watch your back!

t_ch: 这些孽物不是幽囚狱中关押的犯人…
t_en: These abominations aren't prisoners from this prison...

t_ch: 罗浮的监狱管理，真是堪忧…
t_en: The prison management on the Luofu is truly concerning...

t_ch: 你又是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 你们不是正在找我吗？
t_en: Aren't you looking for me?

t_ch: 专心战斗吧。
t_en: Just focus on the fight.

t_ch: 男人仿佛是从监狱的幽暗角落里突然出现，又仿佛下一刻会随时消失。
t_en: The man seems to materialize from the shadowy corners of the prison, as if he could vanish into thin air at any moment.

t_ch: 这个寒鸦口中「身披暗色袍子的亡命之徒」站在你们面前。虽然他冷漠的脸上没有任何表情，但…不知为何，你能读懂那没有任何表情的表情似乎在说「你们终于来了」。
t_en: The "fugitive wearing a dark cloak," spoken of by Hanya, now stands before you. His face is devoid of any expression, cold and indifferent, yet... inexplicably, you could decipher this expressionless facade as if it whispered, "You're finally here."

t_ch: 罗浮的监狱管理，真是堪忧……
t_en: The prison management on the Luofu is truly concerning...

t_ch: 你刚才已经说过了。
t_en: You already said that a moment ago.

t_ch: 这是事实，再说一遍又如何？罗浮的监狱管理，真是堪忧……
t_en: So what if I repeat it? It's the truth after all. The prison management on the Luofu is truly concerning...

t_ch: 你不说话看起来更棒。
t_en: You look better when you're silent.

t_ch: 真的吗？
t_en: Is that so?

t_ch: 你就是那个逃脱的曜青使者！
t_en: Hey, you're the Yaoqing messenger who fled!

t_ch: 是「战略撤退的曜青使者」。
t_en: I prefer to be called "the Yaoqing messenger who strategically retreated."

t_ch: 至少有两拨来历不同的劫狱者闯进了这里，{RUBY_B#步离人}狼崽子们{RUBY_E#}，还有这些魔阴身士卒。
t_en: At least two different groups of intruders infiltrated here — the {RUBY_B#borisin}wolf cubs{RUBY_E#} and these Mara-Struck Soldiers.

t_ch: 这种藏形匿影的技法…和曜青「天风君」座下持明掌握的「风幔」颇为相似。你们有眉目吗？
t_en: Their hiding technique... it's pretty similar to the Wind Mantle technique used by the Vidyadhara serving Caelorum Venti on the Yaoqing. What do you think?

t_ch: 不知道就算了，眼下也不是交流杀手技巧的时候。
t_en: Well, never mind if you don't have any clues. Now is not the time to exchange assassination techniques.

t_ch: 我是貊泽，我的{RUBY_B#椒丘}朋友{RUBY_E#}落在了{RUBY_B#呼雷}头狼{RUBY_E#}的手里。我本打算离开这里尽快报信，但现在看起来……
t_en: I'm Moze. {RUBY_B#Jiaoqiu}My friend{RUBY_E#} fell into the clutches of {RUBY_B#Hoolay}that wolf{RUBY_E#}. I was planning to escape and report back as quickly as possible, but now it seems...

t_ch: 我必须承认，罗浮的监狱设计得很复杂。
t_en: The prison on the Luofu is a real maze, I must admit.

t_ch: 貊泽先生，我们会尽可能保证你朋友的生命安全。
t_en: Thank you for your help, Mr. Moze. We'll do our best to ensure your friend's safety.

t_ch: 你们做不到的。眼下也不应该考虑他的安危。
t_en: No, you can do nothing for him now, and you shouldn't be focusing on his safety at the moment.
